description: This figure presents TEM and HRTEM characterization of the tensile deformation microstructure in a Zr-alloyed Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel (Zr0.50). Subfigure (a) is a bright-field TEM image revealing planar dislocations in the FCC grain interior at an early deformation stage, with an inset SAED pattern along the <110> FCC zone axis. Subfigure (b) shows severe deformation near the fracture surface characterized by dense slip bands along {111} planes, with another <110> FCC SAED pattern. Subfigures (c) and (c1) document deformation nanotwinning: (c) is a TEM image of a nanotwin region containing multiple nanotwins (~20 nm) marked by red arrows, with a composite SAED pattern showing superimposed <110> FCC and <110> Twin zones; (c1) is an atomic-resolution HRTEM image resolving the coherent twin boundary separating FCC matrix and twin. Subfigures (d) and (d1) illustrate precipitation strengthening by nanoscale ZrC particles: (d) is a TEM image showing dislocations interacting with ~30 nm ZrC particles distributed in the FCC matrix; (d1) provides an FFT pattern indexed as <110> ZrC and an atomic-resolution HRTEM lattice image confirming the ZrC carbide structure. Collectively, these images capture the transition from dislocation-mediated plasticity to nanotwinning and ZrC particle pinning, elucidating the multi-mechanism origin of the strength-ductility synergy in this heterogeneous-grain-structured steel.
